sections.title: A: Write one word in each gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence:
Dear Sir,

I would like to (1) ______ a complaint about an article that appeared in your newspaper last week.
In the article, entitled 'Why they are (2) ______ of work', the writer suggested that the unemployed were responsible (3) ______ their own situation.
If the writer had (4) ______ their job properly and (5) ______ a little effort to speak to local unemployed people, they might have realised that this is not the case.
Let me begin (6) ______ saying that most unemployed people want to work.
They apply (7) ______ jobs and (8) ______ a great deal of effort into changing their situation.
The truth is that local businesses are simply not taking (9) ______ new staff.
The government is to blame for the way in which economic growth has (10) ______ down, making life difficult for many businesses.

I look forward to reading a more balanced article in the future.

Yours faithfully,
Rebecca Winterson

sections.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: Dave left the office keys in a pub and he was sacked for being so (RESPONSIBLE) ______.
'Leave it to me. I'll find a solution,' the manager said, (HELP) ______.
All (APPLY) ______ are asked to send their CV to Mrs Simpson in the Personnel Department.
My job is to (SUPERVISE) ______ the telesales workers.
Carol has worked for us for over twenty years with great loyalty and (DEDICATE) ______.
All our (EMPLOY) ______ receive four weeks paid holiday per year.
The managing director didn't think the suggestion would ever be (WORK) ______, so we had to think of another solution.
It seems that Mr Jones lied about his (QUALIFY) ______ on his application form.

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
Yuri qualified and then immediately found a really good job.
than
No ______ he found a really good job.

Women don't often get promoted in some industries.
do
Rarely ______ in some industries.

I never realised that Tony was the head of Fizzco.
realise
Little ______ that Tony was the head of Fizzco.

I didn't know about the meeting until Tracy rang.
did
Not until ______ about the meeting.

I think you will benefit from getting a new job.
good
I think it will be ______ a new job.

The two companies finally agreed the deal.
reached
The two companies finally ______ the deal.

Customers of the bank are never allowed into the basement area.
circumstances
Under ______ customers of the bank allowed into the basement area.

I didn't think of a good answer to the interviewer's question until later.
did
Only ______ a good answer to the interviewer's question.

It was such a boring job that no one wanted to do it.
was
Such ______ that no one wanted to do it.
28 Ironworks Limited has closed
items.match: down and 400 people have lost their jobs.

items.sentence: 29 Henry asked Janet to stand
items.match: in for him while he went into town.

items.sentence: 30 Mr Carter asked me to see
items.match: to the orders while he spoke to a customer.

items.sentence: 31 I heard that Bigburger have taken
items.match: over Cookright and plan to close some branches.

items.sentence: 32 Roger says he never set
items.match: out to become a millionaire - it just happened!

items.sentence: 33 Apparently, Fatfoods are bringing
items.match: out a new flavour of ice cream next month.

items.sentence: 34 I'm thinking of setting
items.match: up a business selling my own jewellery.

items.sentence: The ______ for this position starts at thirty thousand euros per year.
After working at the same factory for thirty years, my grandfather was looking forward to his ______.
Some people ______ to London every day from as far away as Leeds.
Most governments tax people on the amount they ______ each year.
Shelley disagreed with the board's decision and so she ______ and went to work for another company.
When the factory closed, over a hundred people were ______ redundant.
Here at Weatherby's, we provide all our ______, from senior management down, with regular training.
